passedAssertions: VAL-AI-001: GET /llm/status returns both small and large model status fields
VAL-AI-002: POST /autocomplete returns completion or null (existing endpoint preserved)
VAL-AI-003: POST /ai/briefing returns events, pending_reminders, unread_counts, summary fields
VAL-AI-004: POST /ai/triage returns {id: priority} mapping for conversations
VAL-AI-005: POST /ai/summarize returns summary, key_points, action_items, decisions for 5+ msg threads
VAL-AI-006: POST /ai/summarize returns skipped:true for threads with <5 messages
VAL-AI-007: POST /ai/extract-actions returns actions list with text, deadline, type fields
VAL-AI-008: TUI Ctrl+B triggers morning briefing fetch and shows loading status
VAL-AI-009: BriefingModal renders events and pending reminders and dismisses on Escape
VAL-AI-010: ConversationItem renders priority indicators (red for urgent, blue for low, none for normal)
VAL-AI-011: MessageView renders AI summary banner when ai_summary is set on 5+ msg Gmail threads
VAL-AI-012: All 576 tests pass including 53 new AI layer tests; ruff and pyright clean
notes: Large model (3-4B) is never loaded in tests — all large-model paths are mocked via patch.object.
Triage worker is fire-and-forget; test checks crash-free execution not specific priority assignment.
Server briefing endpoint wraps each data source in try/except so partial failures degrade gracefully.
LLM infrastructure extended: _ensure_large_llm_loaded returns bool; llm_large_complete returns None when unavailable.
Summarization only triggers for Gmail threads with 5+ messages; shorter threads return skipped:true.
Action extraction falls back from large model to small model automatically.
